---
title: ""
pagetitle: "Lucia S. Layritz"
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero">

Hi, I am Lucia and I am a climate scientist currently based at UC Berkeley.

&thinsp;

I am broadly interested in modelling the impact of extreme events on the coupled land surface - atmosphere system, especially the impact of wildfire and other ecosystem disturbances on vegetation, and the impact of climate change on ecosystem regeneration.

&thinsp;

My research employs complex Global Dynamic Vegetation Models as well as low- dimensional stochastic dynamical systems, where I am particularly interested in heavy-tailed data and model uncertainty across scales.  

&thinsp;

My work aims to inform strategies for climate adaptation and ecological resilience, and I work closely with land managers and ecologist in U.S. National Parks across the Western US.

```

### Current Position

Postdoctoral Researcher, [Eric & Wendy Schmidt Center for Data Science and Environment](https://dse.berkeley.edu/people/lucia-layritz), UC Berkeley.
Supporting the National Park Service Climate Change Decision Support project.

### Education

- **PhD** Life Science *Technical University of Munich*
- **MSc** Mathematical Modelling *King's College London, UK*
- **MSc** Environmental Science and Engineering *Technical University of Berlin, Germany*
Research stays: Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research · American University of Beirut
